title: Lesson Quiz: Why Governments Form
description: Assessment of the concept that governments form to maintain order, provide protection, support cooperation, and promote the common good.

questions.prompt: Which reason best explains why governments form?

choices.text: To increase confusion and disorder
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments form to reduce disorder, not increase it.

choices.text: To maintain order and support society
choices.feedback: Correct. Maintaining order is one of the main reasons governments form.

choices.text: To eliminate all cooperation among people
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments help people cooperate and work together.

choices.text: To prevent communities from having rules
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments create rules to organize society.

questions.prompt: What does it mean when government promotes the common good?

choices.text: It makes choices that benefit society as a whole
choices.feedback: Correct. The common good refers to benefits for the broader community.

choices.text: It only helps one individual at a time
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The common good focuses on society as a whole, not one person only.

choices.text: It removes all laws from society
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Removing all laws would not support the common good.

choices.text: It avoids making public decisions
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments promote the common good by making decisions for society.

questions.prompt: Why is protection one reason governments form?

choices.text: Because people do not need safety without government
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Safety and protection are key reasons government exists.

choices.text: Because governments help protect citizens from harm
choices.feedback: Correct. Protection and safety are core functions of government.

choices.text: Because protection removes the need for cooperation
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Protection and cooperation can both be reasons governments form.

choices.text: Because only private groups can protect communities
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments also play a major protective role.

choices.text: By encouraging people to work against each other
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments help organize people to work together.

choices.text: By creating shared systems and rules
choices.feedback: Correct. Shared systems and rules help cooperation happen.

choices.text: By preventing communities from organizing services
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments often help organize public services.

choices.text: By removing all expectations for public behavior
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Expectations and rules support cooperation.

questions.prompt: Which statement best reflects the full lesson idea?

choices.text: Governments form only to punish wrongdoing
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The lesson includes order, protection, cooperation, and the common good.

choices.text: Governments form to maintain order, provide protection, support cooperation, and promote the common good
choices.feedback: Correct. This is the complete summary of the lesson.

choices.text: Governments form mainly to avoid responsibility
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments are formed to organize and support society.

choices.text: Governments form so people never need to work together
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Governments support cooperation, not separation.
